channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: News influencers are overwhelmingly male. No wonder there’s a Republican bias
item.description: “News influencers” are social media translators, conveying the day’s biggest stories via TikTok commentary or Instagram graphic. They also happen to be overwhelmingly male. As trust in mainstream media lingers at an all-time low, many are turning to social media apps for their news.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Kotlin for Java developers: Classes and coroutines
item.description: Java is one of the classic object-oriented languages, so classes and objects are of special interest to Java developers. Object-oriented programming gives you considerable power, particularly when it comes to structuring and organizing programs as they grow larger.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia’s rivals are focusing on building AI inference chips. Here’s what to know
item.description: Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New eagle camera is going live in Minnesota to the delight of global viewers
item.description: Eagle lovers around the world who were crushed when the nest of a Minnesota pair and their chick collapsed on live video last year will get a when a new eagle camera goes live this week.
item.dc:creator: Steve Karnowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Study: Chat GPT is better than doctors at diagnosing illness
item.description: A new research study indicates that Open AI’s chatbot Chat GPT-4 is better at diagnosing diseases than human doctors, according to The New York Times. Fifty doctors, a mix of attending physicians and residents, participated in the study; diagnoses were based on evaluations of medical patient cases.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Next Big Things in Tech: Innovative Companies Pushing Boundaries
item.description: Discover the innovative companies making significant contributions to various fields, from solving real-world problems to pioneering new technologies.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: These 3 large companies innovated in a big way in 2024
item.description: While innovation can happen at companies of any size, sometimes it takes a certain scale to get things done. In areas such as autonomous driving and the graphics technology required to represent people more accurately, solutions only come by throwing a whole lot of resources at the problem.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: These AI-assisted innovations are from startups that are five years old or less
item.description: The Googles and Microsofts of the world hardly have a monopoly on innovation. Indeed, a number of recent tech breakthroughs have been delivered from organizations that aren’t even five years old.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: VA approach to automated software testing is rooted in flexibility and efficiency
item.description: "We have approximately 600 applications in the VA and each product line uses their own different automated tools that suits their needs," Coppinger said. The post VA approach to automated software testing is rooted in flexibility and efficiency first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cato Networks adds TLS inspection capabilities to SASE platform
item.description: Secure access service edge provider Cato Networks today announced it added transport layer security inspection capabilities to its SASE platform to enable customers to inspect TLS traffic more easily and quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft Unveils New AI-Powered Tool for Automating Repetitive Tasks
item.description: Microsoft introduces Copilot Actions, a new tool for automating repetitive tasks and making generative AI more useful to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A matter of time: 3 metrics that demonstrate the value of immersion cooling
item.description: Immersion cooling in the data center space gets a lot of positive attention for the formulations that enhance thermal performance, material compatibility, and durability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft and Atom Computing claim breakthrough in reliable quantum computing
item.description: Microsoft and Atom Computing have announced what they claim is a significant step forward in reliable quantum computing, unveiling a commercial quantum machine built with 24 entangled logical qubits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia Blackwell chips face serious heating issues
item.description: Nvidia’s next-generation Blackwell data center processors have significant problems with overheating when installed in high-capacity server racks, forcing redesigns of the racks themselves, according to a report by The Information.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Helpful hack: Customize your home screen by adding widgets on your iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows you how to add, customize and manage widgets for quick access to apps and info from your iPhone home screen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Onion CEO explains Infowars purchase amid Alex Jones's claims about auction
item.description: The Onion CEO Ben Collins confirmed that the publication purchased Alex Jones’s Infowars outlet in a Saturday series of posts on the social media platform Bluesky amid Jones's claims of wrongdoing and illegal activity.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.5 gains security, networking upgrades
item.description: Red Hat is out this week with the latest milestone update of its flagship Linux platform. Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.5 (RHEL) became generally available on Nov.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity
item.description: If left unchecked, Microsoft’s practices will become the industry standard, clearing the path for other providers to harm customers and weaken their defenses. The post Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Exoskeleton helps paralyzed people regain independence
item.description: The WalkON Suit F1, an exoskeleton developed to help people with disabilities, can actually walk over to a person in a wheelchair, solving a major problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says industry was "intensely engaged" in making the new DHS guidelines, and it's up to companies to adopt them. The post DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
